key_insight: A scrutable interface can help teachers steer generative AI outputs for curriculum creation while prompting reflection on their teaching goals and practices.
engineering_relevance: The paper shows how human-centered interface design can make AI tools more controllable, efficient, and usable for K-12 engineering educators.
method: Designed Concept Catalyst: a scrutable interface linked to a generative AI model
Used Wizard-of-Oz testing with middle and high school engineering teachers
Collected interviews, screen/audio recordings, and teacher-produced classroom content
evidence_signal: An exploratory study with 10 depth interviews, averaging 55 minutes each, provided qualitative evidence that the interface improved perceived efficacy, efficiency, and motivation.
watch_item: Results come from a small, exploratory Wizard-of-Oz study, so real-world performance with a fully deployed AI system and broader teacher populations still needs validation.
paper_title: Concept Catalyst: Exploring Scrutable Interfaces to Structure K-12 Teacher Interactions with Generative AI
